title: Runtime ownership closure for Python removal

closure_decision.result: Retain Python as packaged/runtime owner; do not continue automatic Python-removal subgates.
closure_decision.reason: Request-time and packaged-runtime surfaces still require Python backend ownership. Pillow dependency removal is complete and Go has local candidates, but normal Prism workflows still rely on Python for live writes, files/uploads/attachments/delete/cleanup, import/export, prompt/wizard options, migrations/schema/db maintenance, frontend static serving, and rollback ownership.

final_closure.simple_explanation: B is closed by deciding not to pursue Python removal from the current packaged runtime; unresolved no-Python gaps remain retained Python responsibilities, not a queue of automatic new B-next items.
what_this_means: The normal packaged/runtime owner remains Python Flask plus the existing frontend build.
Go local/copied-DB candidates remain evidence and optional future material, not live/default owners.
C. Go packaged runtime release candidate, D. live cutover, and E. Python package deletion are not active follow-ups from this B closure.

classification_rules.go_owned: A surface has a verified Go implementation selected for packaged runtime ownership, with parity, rollback, and package smoke evidence.
classification_rules.removed: A surface is deleted or no longer user-facing/runtime reachable.
classification_rules.external_maintenance_tool: A surface is explicitly outside packaged runtime and must not be required for normal request-time app use.
classification_rules.request_time_blocker: A surface is normal app behavior or package startup/runtime behavior and still requires Python.

surfaces: _thumb.webp generation invoked by retained Python upload/import routes
go_owned_or_package_ready.evidence: Pillow closure smoke uses the built Go artifact with --thumbnail-input / --thumbnail-output and verifies _thumb.webp output.
Implemented in Go and previously live-routed as read-only, but the retained-Python final state keeps Python as the normal owner and the package path has not selected Go as default runtime owner.

external_maintenance_candidates.decision_needed: May be excluded from future no-Python packaged runtime only if docs/UI hide or relabel them as external maintenance tools and package smoke proves normal app workflows do not require them.
May be excluded or replaced by package config files, but current Settings UI still exposes these Python endpoints.

request_time_python_blockers.why_blocking: Core note editing workflow cannot be downgraded out of packaged runtime without removing the app's primary behavior.
Go has local copied-DB update candidates only; live create/delete/merge/cascade behavior remains Python-owned.
Normal editor/media workflows still require Python filesystem mutation, raw file serving, cleanup compensation, and remote-fetch safety behavior.
Backup/export/import and long-content split/restore workflows are user-visible data movement paths, not optional maintenance internals.
Prompt Builder remains a first-class app workflow and still writes through Python.
A no-Python package still needs startup schema safety and runtime DB maintenance/status contracts.

surfaces: Flask V2 SPA serving in current normal runtime
request_time_python_blockers.why_blocking: Go local artifact can serve embedded SPA, but normal package/default runtime ownership and rollback strategy have not been cut over.

unmet_no_python_completion_criteria_clos: Every request_time_python_blocker would need to be implemented in Go with parity tests and package smoke, removed from the product surface, or explicitly downgraded outside packaged runtime without breaking normal workflows.
External maintenance candidates would need to be hidden, relabeled, replaced, or documented so normal packaged runtime does not call Python.
A no-Python packaged runtime would need to start, migrate or validate schema safely, serve SPA, and support normal note/search/editor/media/import/export workflows without Python backend.
Pi/live route ownership and rollback evidence would need to confirm no request-time packaged surface depends on Python.
not_authorized_by_B_assessment: Start C. Go packaged runtime release candidate
Start D. Live cutover and rollback proof

executed_notes_bundle_candidate.title: Notes write/actions/history/batch packaged-runtime ownership bundle
executed_notes_bundle_candidate.why_first: It is the primary request-time blocker. Without note create/update/delete/actions/history/batch, a no-Python package cannot preserve Prism's core workflow.
scope: Implement or explicitly retain/downgrade notes core writes/actions/history/batch as one ownership bundle.
Use copied DB fixtures first; no production DB, Pi, Caddy, or systemd changes.
Require Python vs Go response and DB-state parity, rollback/no-partial-write assertions, and package smoke before any live route promotion.
executed_notes_bundle_candidate.final_role: historical local candidate only; not a trigger for more B-next work

executed_notes_promotion_decision.title: Notes bundle live/default ownership decision and media-cleanup boundary
executed_notes_promotion_decision.decision: Do not promote the B-next.1 notes bundle to live/default owner yet.
executed_notes_promotion_decision.reason: The notes bundle remains copied-DB only and note deletion media cleanup remains Python-owned.
executed_notes_promotion_decision.simple_explanation: B-next.2 closed the notes promotion decision; the final B closure now stops the automatic B-next chain entirely.
